preview.summary: Defending champions Argentina face a disciplined, Red Bull-schooled Austria in Group J. Lionel Scaloni's side blend Qatar-winning experience with fresh legs, while Ralf Rangnick's pressing Austrians aim to upset a favorite. A contrast of star quality against structured collective.
preview.homeForm: Ranked 3rd, Argentina topped CONMEBOL qualifying comfortably as reigning world champions.
preview.awayForm: Ranked 24th, Austria topped their group with six wins to reach a first World Cup since 1998.
preview.homeKey: Lautaro Martinez, in clinical form as Scaloni's number nine.
preview.awayKey: Marko Arnautovic, record scorer who netted eight qualifying goals.
storylines: Argentina chase back-to-back titles, last done by Brazil in 1962
Messi leads at a record sixth World Cup
Rangnick's Austria back on the big stage after 28 years
preview.h2h: The sides have met only a handful of times, with Argentina holding the historical edge in past friendlies.
